title: Activity: Debug & Fix
description: Analyse and repair broken fetch code in a series of guided tasks.

blocks.title: What to expect
blocks.content: In this activity you will be given broken fetch code and asked to identify and fix the problem. Read each task carefully before editing the code.
Use the working example below as a reference for all tasks in this activity.

blocks.title: Working example
Background
blocks.content: A teammate's fetch function is printing [object Promise] instead of the actual quote text. They forgot something important.

blocks.title: Instructions
blocks.content: Find and fix the bug so that the quote text is printed correctly.

blocks.title: Task 1

blocks.body: Look carefully at where the await keywords are used.

blocks.title: Background
blocks.content: A co-worker is trying to print the title of product #5 but keeps getting undefined. The API is working fine.

blocks.title: Instructions
blocks.content: Fix the property access so the correct product title is printed.

blocks.title: Task 2

blocks.body: JSON keys are case-sensitive. Try logging the full data object to see the actual key names.

blocks.title: Background
blocks.content: This code should fetch user number 3 but always hits an error. The error handler is correctly in place — the URL just needs to be fixed.

blocks.title: Instructions
blocks.content: Fix the URL so the user's first and last name are printed.

blocks.title: Task 3

blocks.body: Check the DummyJSON docs — is the endpoint /user or /users?
